prompt:
One-line-ish change: declare a second project on classic-laddie's valley host. In hosts/classic-laddie/valley.cue, add to the projects block:

  "qinling": {}

with a short comment in the file's existing style: qinling is the gunk-dev valley *instance* repo — the instance's own declaration, runbook, and operational outcomes; born sovereign (canonical origin is this host; deliberately NO mirrors yet — a GitHub mirror gets declared later, when cosmo consumes the instance declaration as a flake input and CI needs a fetchable copy). Name context (one clause is plenty): Qinling, the valley range associated with the Tao Te Ching's origin.

Validate: cue vet -c against the-valley's schema at cosmo's locked rev (empty project value is valid — git.enable defaults true, mirrors default []); nix flake check; nix eval .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel.drvPath. Touch nothing else. PR body: what qinling is (the instance repo per the owner's decision), that merging auto-deploys via the now-working rebuild loop and valley-init will create the empty bare repo git@classic-laddie:qinling.git, and that content seeding follows as a separate step in S1 direct-push mode.
